flashcards.definition: Stating statistical conclusions in terms of the real-world problem, not just statistical jargon.
Association (correlation) means variables are related; causation means one variable causes changes in another.
Whether a statistically significant result is large enough to matter in the real world.
The extent to which results from a sample can be applied to the broader population.
Acknowledged weaknesses of a study, including assumptions, potential biases, and scope of conclusions.
